text:
Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Olive who loved collecting owl feathers. She discovered an old owl book at her grandmother's that told tales of wise owls like/owlswoman owl*. Every night she'd listen to their stories on her favorite recording.

One particular feather was so lovely, a deep midnight blue. Olive lost it one rainy afternoon playing outside. She looked everywhere, calling owl voices.

Days turned into weeks. Olive grew upset. Then she heard them — an old owl! He swooped down. "Oh dear," he said. "That feather? You lost it!"

"Yes!"

He thought for a moment. "Well, how about this?"

*The owl spoke in rhyme.*

"Every night before you sleep?"

"Always!"

"I'll give you my brightest. Catch them."

Then the owl just gave her a big old flap.*

"Ow!"

"He likes flies."

So every night Olive fed the owl his shiny feather. You know?

*One night.*
